obligations: Employer shall employ Employee as Director of Operations
Employer shall pay base salary of $185,000 per annum semi-monthly
Employer shall pay target bonus of 20% of base salary no later than March 31 each year
Employer shall provide written notice of termination without cause
Employee shall provide no less than 30 days prior written notice of resignation
Employee must immediately notify Employer of unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information
Employee shall report directly to the Chief Executive Officer and perform duties assigned from time to time
Employee shall devote substantially all working time to duties and shall not engage in outside employment that conflicts with obligations without prior written consent
During the notice period, Employee shall continue to perform duties and cooperate with the transition of responsibilities
Employer shall first provide Employee with written notice specifying the alleged deficiency before terminating for cause based on performance
Employee shall have 15 business days after receipt of written notice to cure the deficiency to the reasonable satisfaction of the Employer

During the term of employment and for a period of 12 months following termination, Employee shall not solicit any customer of Sandbourne Industries Inc. with whom Employee had material contact during the 24 months preceding termination
During the term of employment and for a period of 6 months following termination, Employee shall not solicit, hire, or attempt to hire any employee of Sandbourne Industries Inc.
Employee agrees to keep confidential all Confidential Information of Sandbourne Industries Inc. both during and after the term of employment
Employer shall make all deductions required by law, including income tax, Canada Pension Plan contributions, and Employment Insurance premiums
Employer shall provide Employee with group benefits coverage, including extended health, dental, and life insurance
deadlines: target bonus of 20% of base salary payable no later than March 31 of each calendar year following the performance year
no less than 30 days prior written notice (employee resignation)
no later than the effective date of termination (notice of termination without cause)
15 business days after receipt of such written notice to cure the deficiency
Employer may terminate for cause if Employee fails to cure within 15-business-day period
conditions: Employee remaining employed as of bonus payment date
material breach of Agreement by Employee
willful misconduct, fraud, or dishonesty in performance of duties
failure to cure performance deficiency within 15-business-day cure period
Employer provides written notice specifying performance deficiency before termination for cause
